description: Structured knowledge of heap exploitation techniques extracted from C source analysis, covering glibc 2.34-2.39

techniques.name: Tcache Poisoning
techniques.description: Overwrite tcache chunk's fd pointer to achieve arbitrary allocation. Most fundamental tcache technique.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Write to freed chunk fd pointer, then two consecutive allocs of same size

techniques.name: Unsorted Bin Leak
techniques.description: Free a large chunk (> tcache max) into unsorted bin, read its fd/bk to leak main_arena address.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Alloc large chunk, alloc guard, free large, view/read on freed large chunk yields libc address

techniques.name: Fastbin Double-Free
techniques.description: Fill tcache (7 frees), then double-free into fastbin to get overlapping chunk pointers.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Many allocs+frees of same size, then free(a)-free(b)-free(a) pattern
pattern_hints.required_operations: Must be able to free same index multiple times

techniques.name: Fastbin Dup via Consolidate
techniques.description: Fill tcache, free to fastbin, trigger malloc_consolidate (large alloc) to merge fastbin chunk, then free again to get overlapping pointers.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Fill tcache (7 frees), free to fastbin, then trigger large alloc causing consolidation. The solve uses large scanf input for this.
pattern_hints.trigger: Large malloc (>=0x400) or large scanf input triggers malloc_consolidate

techniques.name: Fastbin Double-Free into Stack
techniques.description: Extends fastbin_dup: after dup, overwrite fd to point to fake stack chunk. Subsequent callocs return stack address.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Fastbin dup followed by writing controlled value to chunk fd

techniques.name: Fastbin Reverse into Tcache
techniques.description: Fill tcache, put chunks in fastbin, poison one fd, then empty tcache to trigger fastbin-to-tcache refill, placing poisoned entry at tcache head.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Fill tcache (7), alloc many fastbin-sized chunks, free to fastbin, write to one's fd

techniques.name: House of Botcake
techniques.description: Create tcache+unsorted bin double-free by filling tcache, freeing victim to unsorted bin, freeing prev (backward consolidation merges), then freeing victim again to tcache.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: 7 filler allocs + prev+ victim + guard layout, fill tcache, free victim, free prev (consolidation), take from tcache, free victim again

techniques.name: House of Einherjar
techniques.description: Off-by-one null byte overflow to corrupt prev_inuse and prev_size, creating fake chunk that passes unlink during backward consolidation.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Off-by-one null byte overflow into next chunk's size/prev_size fields

techniques.name: House of Lore
techniques.description: Corrupt smallbin bk pointer to point to stack fake chunk. Draining tcache + allocating from smallbin returns fake chunk from stack.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Free large chunk into smallbin via sorting, corrupt bk pointer

techniques.name: House of Mind (Fastbin)
techniques.description: Use many large allocs to reach next heap region boundary, place fake heap_info there, set NON_MAIN_ARENA bit, then free to write to controlled offset via fake arena fastbin array.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Many large allocations to layout heap regions

techniques.name: House of Spirit
techniques.description: Create fake chunk on stack with valid size header, free the fake pointer to add it to free list. Next alloc returns stack address.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Free a pointer pointing to controlled stack area

techniques.name: Tcache House of Spirit
techniques.description: Simplified House of Spirit for tcache: create fake chunk on stack, free it. Tcache accepts it without checking next chunk's size.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Free a pointer to stack/controlled memory with fake size header

techniques.name: House of Tangerine
techniques.description: Corrupt top chunk size (via OOB write), then large alloc triggers sysmalloc which frees old top chunk into attacker-chosen size bin. Repeated to get tcache poisoning.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Overwrite top chunk size, then large alloc triggers free of old top

techniques.name: House of Water
techniques.description: Use UAF on smallbin chunks to corrupt fd/bk LSB. Smallbin stashing moves corrupted entries to tcache, giving tcache_perthread_struct control.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: UAF write to LSB of smallbin chunk fd/bk, then calloc to trigger stashing

techniques.name: Large Bin Attack
techniques.description: Overwrite a large chunk's bk_nextsize to point to target-0x20. When a smaller chunk is sorted into largebin, a heap pointer is written to target.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Two large allocs of different sizes, free both, write to bk_nextsize of the larger one

techniques.name: Overlapping Chunks (Unsorted Bin)
techniques.description: Corrupt freed chunk's size to a larger value. When re-allocated with the larger size, the new chunk overlaps adjacent chunks.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Write to freed chunk's size field to enlarge it

techniques.name: Mmap Overlapping Chunks
techniques.description: Corrupt mmap'd chunk size to include adjacent mmap chunks. Munmap releases both, next large mmap overlaps the old region.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Very large allocs (>= 0x200000), corrupt size of one to include another

techniques.name: Poison Null Byte
techniques.description: Off-by-one null byte overflow to clear prev_inuse of next chunk. Backward consolidation with carefully crafted fake chunk overlaps memory.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Off-by-one null byte overflow into next chunk's size field LSB

techniques.name: Unsafe Unlink
techniques.description: Create fake free chunk inside an allocated chunk with crafted fd/bk that satisfy unlink checks (P->fd->bk == P && P->bk->fd == P). Freeing adjacent chunk triggers unlink, overwriting a pointer.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Fake chunk setup inside allocated memory, then free adjacent chunk to trigger consolidation

techniques.name: Tcache Metadata Poisoning
techniques.description: Directly overwrite tcache_perthread_struct (counts and entries) by calculating offset from controlled heap chunk to the struct at heap start.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Overflow from heap chunk backwards to overwrite tcache_perthread_struct at heap base

techniques.name: Tcache Relative Write
techniques.description: Use large_bin_attack to overwrite mp_.tcache_bins, then arbitrary size alloc causes tcache_idx to overflow, writing freed pointer to arbitrary heap location via tcache_put.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Requires large_bin_attack + subsequent alloc with very specific size

techniques.name: Tcache Stashing Unlink Attack
techniques.description: Corrupt smallbin bk pointer. When calloc triggers tcache stashing from smallbin, fake chunk is linked into tcache and a libc address is written to fake_chunk->bk+0x10.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Corrupt smallbin chunk bk pointer, then calloc (not malloc) triggers stashing

techniques.name: Safe-Link Double Protect
techniques.description: Point one tcache bin's entry to another bin's safe-linked pointer. When allocated, the value is XOR'd again, recovering the original plaintext pointer.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Write tcache entries to point to each other's safe-linked pointers

techniques.name: Decrypt Safe-Linking
techniques.description: Recover plaintext pointer from XOR-encrypted (safe-linked) tcache/fastbin fd pointer. Uses iterative algorithm exploiting known lower 12 bits.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Read first 8 bytes of freed tcache chunk, contains mangled heap pointer

techniques.name: sysmalloc_int_free (Top Chunk Free)
techniques.description: Corrupt top chunk size to be page-aligned, then large alloc triggers sysmalloc which calls _int_free on the old top chunk into an attacker-chosen bin.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Corrupt top chunk size, then large alloc; foundation for House of Orange/Tangerine

techniques.name: Environ Stack Leak
techniques.description: Use arbitrary read to read __environ from libc BSS. __environ points to the stack envp array, giving a stack address for ROP targeting.

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Tcache poison to libc environ address, then read the target chunk

techniques.name: Malloc Consolidate Trigger
techniques.description: Trigger malloc_consolidate by sending large input to scanf or by allocating a large chunk (>= FASTBIN_CONSOLIDATION_THRESHOLD). Merges fastbin chunks into unsorted bin.
produces: libc_leak (via consolidation of previously freed chunks)

pattern_hints.observable_trace: Sending large input to a scanf-based menu option triggers internal malloc; or allocating large chunk
pattern_hints.note: When fastbin chunks are consolidated to unsorted bin, their fd/bk contain libc addresses

safe_linking.description: XOR encoding of tcache/fastbin fd pointers (chunk_addr >> 12). Introduced in glibc 2.32.

tcache_double_free_detection.description: Tcache stores a key (chunk address) in freed chunk's second qword. Free checks if key matches, detects double-free.

hook_removal.description: __free_hook and __malloc_hook removed in glibc 2.34. Alternative targets required.

consolidation_checks.description: Glibc validates prev_inuse, prev_size, and unlink integrity during backward consolidation.
